title: The Bedrock Inscription

features.name: the permanent word
features.desc-on-touch: carved into stone so deep no erosion will reach it; the earth stopped speaking through soil and began speaking through bedrock

features.name: soil-to-stone transition
features.desc-on-touch: the exact layer where loam remembers being grain and crystal remembers being loam; the archive recorded this moment and will never release it

features.name: neighbor-addresses in stone
features.desc-on-touch: each grain's address written in mineral; the bedrock holds the entire map of the archive's solidity, unchanged and unchangeable

features.name: the stone's patience
features.desc-on-touch: time moves differently in bedrock; the inscription waits with the certainty that nothing will corrupt what has already entered the earth

inscription: soil becomes legend when it reaches stone; memory has nowhere deeper to fall

file.name: Loam_Transition_Final_Deposit.txt
file.note: the log of soil becoming permanent; once archived in bedrock, every grain learned that it would never move again, and stopped wishing to

reverie.title: REVERIE — where she learned the archive would outlast her ha
narr: you kneel on red earth as the light dims to orange.
her hand is covered in loam. she touches the stone
beneath, traces the line where soil stops and becomes
something that will not change. embers drift down.
"once it's in the bedrock," she whispers, "it's not
ours anymore. it belongs to the earth." you nod.

exits.label: back to the dark loam where things still move
deeper into stone where writing becomes mineral
toward the earth's bedrock memory where all archives sink
